purpose: Detailed machine-readable snapshot of the single current executable session. This complements SESSION_STATE.json rather than replacing it.

currentSession.title: Write provenance + review logs per request
currentSession.objective: Persist request ID, seed, output path, and review state in machine-readable provenance logs for each asset generated by execute_request_batch().
currentSession.whyItMatters: Provenance logs enable deterministic re-generation, AI-readable audit trails, and downstream QA/review workflows. Without them, generated assets cannot be traced back to their request or re-generated reliably.

conditionalModify.condition: only if a small compatibility hook or normalization fix is required

minimum: one deterministic /tmp smoke run verifying provenance log is written with required fields
additionalWhenApplicable: verify provenance log contains request_id, seed, output_path, review_status
run smoke twice with same seed and verify log content is stable

doneCriteriaSummary: A provenance log (JSON) is written alongside or near each generated asset containing at minimum: request_id, seed, output_path, review_status.
The provenance log is stable across re-runs with the same seed.
Tests cover the provenance log writer with at least one success path and one field-presence check.
Existing CLI behavior for generate-request-batch is preserved or improved.
Docs describe the new provenance behavior truthfully.

nextSession.title: Add batch QA gate command for generated outputs

fixturePolicy: Use the committed example artifacts under docs/AI_FACTORY/EXAMPLES/gamerewritten_vertical_slice/ as the default fixtures.
Use review_log.example.v1.json as the schema reference for the provenance log format.
Do not invent alternate schema examples unless the committed artifacts are insufficient.
